instruction: In ship2, finish the bootstrapped app by adding the minimal smoke test/setup needed so local verification passes. Keep the existing UI shell. Make sure `npm run build` and `npm test` both pass in /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship2.

steps.description: Bootstrap the missing app manifest and TypeScript/Vite config from the existing lockfile/build artifacts so the repository has the minimal install/build/test entrypoints required for local verification. Define npm scripts for build and test, include vite/react/typescript deps already reflected in package-lock, and add vitest plus a browser-like test environment dependency/config.

Recreate the minimal Vite React app shell entry files while preserving the existing UI shell structure inferred from the built output: HTML entrypoint, React mount file, root App component, and shell styling. Keep the app simple and stable so the smoke test can assert the shell renders without introducing extra behavior.

Add the minimal Vitest smoke-test setup needed for local verification: configure test bootstrap helpers, create a single smoke test that renders the existing UI shell and asserts key shell content is present, and ensure imports use node16-compatible .js extensions where applicable in source/tests.
Update the npm lockfile to reflect the restored package manifest and newly added test dependencies/scripts so `npm run build` and `npm test` can run successfully from the repo root.
